previousGrants.title: AI in logistics systems for national security

additionalInfo:
UNIVERSITY PROFILE:

Zaporizhzhia National University (ZNU) is a major Ukrainian university with 18 faculties, an Engineering Institute, and 4 colleges. It has 10 buildings, a modern sports complex, 6 halls of residence, and extensive research infrastructure.

KEY RESEARCH UNIT: Laboratory of Parallel and Distributed Computing
- Head: Dr. Halyna Shylo, Doctor of Technical Sciences
- Team: 4 core researchers (1 PhD, 1 Professor)
- Expertise: HPC, Parallel Computing, AI/ML, Distributed Systems
- Publications: 25 total (8 in last year), Scopus-indexed
- Current project: AI in logistics systems for national security (3.59M UAH state budget)
- International partnerships: Durham University (UK), TU Bergakademie Freiberg (Germany)

UNIVERSITY CAPABILITIES:
- 18 faculties covering STEM, humanities, economics, law, education, biology, physics, mathematics
- Engineering Institute (formerly Zaporizhzhia State Engineering Academy)
- Multiple academic journals (Bulletin of ZNU series: Biology, Physics/Math, Economics, Pedagogy, etc.)
- Scientific library with 1M+ copies and electronic library
- Student research society and undergraduate/postgraduate research programs
- Confucius School, Polish Language Centre, Shakespeare Centre, Goethe-Institut partnerships
- Faculties in multiple cities (Kriviy Rih, Melitopol)

ELIGIBILITY:
- Ukraine is associated with Horizon Europe (since 2021)
- Ukraine is associated with Digital Europe Programme
- Ukrainian HEIs are eligible for EIT initiatives
- ZNU is accredited by Ukrainian national authorities

STRENGTHS AS UNIVERSITY APPLICANT:
1. Higher Education Institution (HEI) — eligible as lead in education-focused grants
2. Multi-disciplinary — 18 faculties enable cross-cutting proposals
3. STEM education capacity — physics, math, CS, engineering faculties
4. International academic partnerships (UK, Germany)
5. Active research projects and Scopus publications
6. Student body for training/education-focused grants
7. Engineering Institute for applied research
